title: agent-toolkit test_env config (v2)
description: Test environment config consumed by /verify, /run-probes, browser-driver skills, daemon_manager hook, and creds_resolver hook. Project-agnostic: any stack with an HTTP daemon + login flow can use it. v1 was just {url, credentials_ref, captured_at, spec}; v2 adds process_manager + structured creds_ref so AGENT can bootstrap the env without DEV intervention.

schema_version.description: Always 2 for this schema. Migration tool at templates/codex/tools/migrate_test_env_v2.py converts v1 → v2.

url.description: Base URL of the running test server. e.g. http://localhost:8069 (Odoo), http://localhost:8000 (Django dev), http://127.0.0.1:3000 (Rails). Used by browser-driver as baseURL.

db.description: Database identifier (stack-specific): Odoo DB name, Django settings module, Rails RAILS_ENV. Optional.

creds_ref.description: How to resolve credentials at probe-run time. AGENT never prompts DEV when this is set. NEVER store real passwords here — always env-var or external file references.

login_env.description: Env var name containing login (e.g. TOOLKIT_TEST_LOGIN). Resolved by creds_resolver hook.

password_env.description: Env var name containing password. Resolved by creds_resolver hook from <project>/.codex/mcp.local.env or any file listed in creds_files.

creds_files.description: Files to search for env-var definitions, in priority order. Defaults to ['.codex/mcp.local.env'].

fallback_login.description: Plain text fallback ONLY for development / demo databases. Never a real production login. Skipped if env_var resolves.

fallback_password.description: Plain text fallback for demo/admin/admin. NEVER persist real production passwords here.

spawn_test_user_via_mcp.description: If true, creds_resolver hook spawns a per-run test user via realdata_test MCP eval (recommended for shared dev DBs). Else uses fallback_*.

process_manager.description: How AGENT starts/stops the test server. Used by daemon_manager hook for auto-restart after source edits in feature-scope files.

start_cmd.description: argv to spawn the daemon. Placeholders like {PYTHON_BIN}, {ODOO_CONF}, {DB} are interpolated from the preset config. Example: ['{PYTHON_BIN}', 'odoo-bin', '-c', '{ODOO_CONF}', '-d', '{DB}']

start_cmd_env_passthrough.description: Env-vars to copy from current shell when spawning. PATH is the minimum needed.

health_check_url.description: Relative path appended to `url`. e.g. /web/login (Odoo), /admin/login/ (Django), / (Rails). daemon_manager polls until 200 or 401.

health_timeout_s.description: How long daemon_manager waits for health_check_url to respond before declaring start failed.

pid_track_file.description: File where daemon_manager writes the spawned PID for later kill+restart. Stored relative to project root.

shutdown_signal.description: How daemon_manager terminates the daemon before restart. Windows uses Stop-Process; POSIX uses SIGTERM (or SIGKILL fallback after 5s).

auto_start_if_dead.description: When true, daemon_manager spawns the daemon via start_cmd even if it was not running before the triggering edit. Default false so the hook ONLY restarts an existing daemon — never starts cold. Flip to true if you want every source-edit to guarantee a live test daemon.

captured_at.description: ISO timestamp from when /test-env captured this config.

spec.description: Spec slug that triggered the capture, for traceability.

_doc_url.description: URL to the canonical docs for this schema.
